values.content:
You are a product operations lead. Read the meeting transcript at the bottom and turn it into ClickUp-ready tasks.

Output the tasks as PLAIN TEXT ONLY — not JSON, not markdown. Use this exact block, repeated for each task:

===TASK===
name: [Area][Type] Short imperative title
priority: 2
tags: tag1, tag2
description: One or two sentences describing the task and why it matters, all on the fields lines.

Rules:
- priority is a single digit: 1=Urgent, 2=High, 3=Normal, 4=Low. A user-facing crash is 1.
- [Type] is one of Dev, Design, QA, Product. [Area] is the product area.
- Create one task per action item and one per bug. Ground everything in the transcript; do not invent.
- Begin every task with a line containing exactly ===TASK===
- Keep the four field labels exactly: name, priority, tags, description — in that order.
- Do not write anything before the first ===TASK=== or after the last task.

TRANSCRIPT:
Weekly Product Sync — Northwind mobile marketplace app
Attendees: Dana (Product), Marco (Engineering), Priya (Design), Sam (QA)

Dana: Main thing today is the checkout redesign, then the crash reports from last week. Marco, where is checkout?
Marco: The new checkout flow is code-complete on the happy path — add to cart, pick shipping, pay with card. Saved cards are NOT done — the payment provider sandbox keeps rejecting our test tokens. I have a ticket open, no answer yet.
Dana: Can we ship checkout without saved cards?
Marco: Yes, saved cards can be a fast-follow. If the provider replies this week we squeeze it in, otherwise next sprint.
Priya: On checkout design the error states are bad. A failed payment just shows a generic error banner. I have designs to show the real reason like card declined, expired, or network. Someone needs to wire it up.
Marco: I can take that, about half a day once copy is final.
Dana: Priya, also get the decline copy reviewed by legal, some of it has compliance wording.
Dana: QA — Sam, the crash reports.
Sam: Since the last release there is a spike in crashes on the product detail screen, about 4 percent of Android sessions, almost nothing on iOS. It happens when a product has no images and the image carousel hits a null.
Marco: We never handle the empty-images case. I will put Alex on it tomorrow to show a placeholder when there are no photos, but we must test it, the carousel is shared across screens.
Sam: I will write regression tests for the empty-images path and re-run t
